class.name: Terrain3DStreamer
class.description: This class manages runtime region streaming. Regions around the clipmap target are loaded on background threads and evicted as the target moves away, so memory scales with the loaded area instead of the whole terrain. It works with the slot pool in [Terrain3DData]; see [method Terrain3DData.enable_streaming]. Settings are exposed as aliases on the [Terrain3D] node (e.g. [member Terrain3D.streaming_enabled]), which is also where they are saved. Streaming only runs outside the editor; the editor always keeps the full terrain loaded.

method.name: abort_pending
method.description: Consumes and drops every in flight threaded load. An unconsumed threaded load stays pinned in the ResourceLoader forever, so this runs whenever streaming stops: rescans, disabling streaming at runtime, and leaving the tree.

method.name: get_max_distance
method.description: The largest [member distance] the current [member slots] capacity can hold with the current [member shape].

method.name: get_required_slots
method.description: How many pool slots the given distance needs with the current [member shape], including the one region hysteresis band. Square: (2 * (distance + 1) + 1)².

method.name: get_stats
method.description: Returns live streaming counters: [code skip-lint]streaming_active[/code], [code skip-lint]resident[/code], [code skip-lint]free_slots[/code], [code skip-lint]inflight[/code], [code skip-lint]failed[/code], [code skip-lint]known[/code], [code skip-lint]landed_total[/code], [code skip-lint]evicted_total[/code], and [code skip-lint]land_ms_avg[/code] (an exponential moving average of an insertion's main thread cost in ms). Useful for debug overlays and tests.

method.name: is_active
method.description: Returns [code skip-lint]true[/code] when streaming is enabled and the game is running outside the editor.

method.name: is_location_known
method.description: Returns [code skip-lint]true[/code] when a region file exists on disk at [param location]. The set is seeded by the boot directory scan and kept current as regions are saved, created, or deleted, so it stays authoritative for the editor overwrite guards and the streaming dock's on-disk cells.

method.name: scan_directory
method.description: Scans the data directory for region locations and resets streaming state. Only file names are read, nothing is loaded. Called automatically at initialization while streaming is active; call it manually to refresh the known-set after region files change on disk outside the streamer.

method.name: step
method.description: Runs the streaming state machine once: evicts regions that fell outside the loaded area, inserts completed loads, and requests missing regions. Called automatically once per physics frame while streaming is active.

members.member: How many regions may be loading on background threads at once. Loading runs wide while insertions stay narrow; see [member loads_per_frame].

How far the loaded area extends from the clipmap target, in regions. For example, 4 with a square [member shape] keeps a 9x9 block of regions loaded (the target region plus 4 in every direction); a circle of the same radius keeps fewer. In world units the radius is distance x region_size x vertex_spacing. Higher values load more of the world at once and cost more memory. Raising distance automatically raises [member slots] to fit, so it just works; lowering slots below what the current distance needs pulls distance back down (with a warning). The maximum is bounded by the 1024 slot ceiling (square shape tops out at distance 14).

Enables region streaming at runtime. Instead of loading every region up front, regions within [member distance] of the clipmap target are loaded on background threads and evicted as the target moves away. Regions modified at runtime are saved back to the data directory before eviction. Has no effect inside the editor; see [method is_active].

What eviction does with a region's data. DISK saves modified regions and drops them; RAM keeps every body cached in memory (and prefetches the rest of the world in the background with spare load budget), so only the GPU layers follow the loaded area and revisits never touch the disk. RAM mode trades memory for the smoothest possible region changes. Modified cached regions are saved when leaving RAM mode or disabling streaming. Alias target of [member Terrain3D.streaming_mode].

How many completed region loads may be inserted into the terrain per physics frame. Each insertion uploads texture layers and updates collision on the main thread, so raising this trades hitching for faster fill in.
The shape of the loaded area around the clipmap target: a square block or a circle of radius [member distance]. A circle loads ~21% fewer regions at the same distance.

When [code skip-lint]true[/code], a region loaded from an older data version is not restamped to the current version on insertion. Restamping marks the region modified, so without this a session that merely pans over an old world writes every region back to disk as it evicts. With it, a region upgrades and saves only when a real edit modifies it.

Capacity of the pooled texture arrays, in regions. Sized automatically when you raise [member distance] (the pool must hold every region within distance + 1 for hysteresis: (2 * (distance + 1) + 1)² for a square shape, so 25 at distance 1, 121 at distance 4). Set it directly only to reserve extra headroom; setting it below what the current distance needs clamps distance down. Changing it while streaming rebuilds the terrain data at the new capacity.

constants.constant: The loaded area is a square block of regions around the clipmap target.
The loaded area is a circle of regions around the clipmap target.
Evicted regions save (if modified) and drop; RAM and GPU both follow the loaded area.
Region bodies stay cached in RAM; only the GPU layers follow the loaded area.
